Sapros.definition: Greek — rotten, decayed, putrid. The title of this story. Refers to what Mika is becoming, and what the Council already is.

Eques.definition: The Council's enforcement officers. Five ranks: Thebes → Skiritai → Achaean → Agema → Hetairoi. The Eques are the only branch of society exempt from civilian surveillance — the privilege Mika is trading his old life for.

Thebes.definition: First Eques rank (age 15). Recruits doing menial labor — moving crates, mopping facilities, processing the lowest paperwork. No combat assignments, no weapons.

Skiritai.definition: Second Eques rank (age 16). Field officers. Street patrol, detentions, checkpoint duty. First rank authorized to carry a service rifle and use force.

Achaean.definition: Third Eques rank (age 17). Squad leader. Oversees Skiritai units and personally carries out targeted operations against citizens the Council has flagged.

Agema.definition: Fourth Eques rank (age 18). Commander. Signs kill orders that come down from the Council. One step below the Hetairoi.

Hetairoi.definition: The Council itself. Five members, age 19 or older. They read flagged citizens' files and decide who lives, dies, or quietly disappears. Mika's destination if he climbs all the way.

Capitol.definition: The walled inner city — seat of the Council and Eques operations. Where the wealthy live, where the surveillance is densest, and where Mika now reports to work.

Quadrant.definition: One of four districts outside the Capitol wall, where most citizens live. Every transit movement, every search, every conversation in the Quadrants is logged by the Council's predictive system.

Council.definition: The ruling body of the city. Five Hetairoi who meet to review flagged citizens and authorize action. Their decisions are final and not appealable.

Meditations.definition: A real 2,000-year-old book by the Roman emperor Marcus Aurelius. Personal reflections on duty, mortality, and the discipline of staying ethical inside a system that doesn't reward ethics. Jason gives Mika a paperback copy on his first night as an Achaean.
